techMovers.explanation: Oracle dropped on broader tech weakness and concerns that cloud infrastructure spending may decelerate amid corporate budget tightening.

techMovers.explanation: Coinbase declined as Bitcoin slid 1.45% and broader crypto weakness suppressed trading volume and fee revenue expectations.

pulsePicks.reasoning: Visa is up 1.11% against a broadly red tape, demonstrating clear relative strength. The new onchain banking partnership with Tether co-founder Reeve Collins via WeFi positions Visa at the intersection of traditional payments and crypto infrastructure. Fear & Greed at 33 historically creates favorable entry points for quality compounders with durable moats.

NVIDIA is down 3.35% to $209.36 but structural AI infrastructure demand remains intact — Galaxy Digital just delivered its first Helios data center tranche to CoreWeave, validating ongoing buildout. The pullback may create a tactical entry, but semiconductor sector headwinds and macro uncertainty warrant waiting for sector stabilization before adding.

Bitcoin sits at $75,913 with Fear & Greed at 33 (fear zone). Veteran analyst Peter Brandt warns the bear phase may not be over with a potential drop to $57K by October, while AML regulatory tightening across the US and Japan adds compliance headwinds. Wide analyst divergence argues for patience before increasing exposure.
